champion.narrative: Carolyn Hartzell (Senior Purchasing Manager) is the single engaged stakeholder and the developing champion. She initiated contact, drove the discovery and demo calls (2026-05-06, 2026-06-10, 2026-06-12, 2026-06-16), articulated pain and requirements, and committed to building the business case for CFO presentation. However, her champion strength is weak: she has no demonstrated access to the Economic Buyer (CFO engagement is deferred, not active), no evidence of internal advocacy beyond her own evaluation, and no multi-threading to IT, finance, or executive stakeholders despite multi-site implementation and ERP integration scope. Her engagement has cooled since the 2026-06-16 demo: 13 days have passed with no two-way touch, and the intake form she is filling out (as of 2026-06-29 Next Step) remains incomplete. She is an evaluator, not a coach or internal advocate. Developing status reflects her ownership of the evaluation process but absence of power, access, and urgency to drive the deal forward.

competition.narrative: Tradogram is the incumbent and primary competitor. Carolyn stated on the 2026-05-06 discovery call that Thompson Pipe has been using Tradogram for procurement but is dissatisfied with the integration complexity and lack of implementation support (Tradogram required Thompson Pipe to lead a 60-page integration spec, which they could not execute). Carolyn also mentioned on the same call that she planned to evaluate Coupa as part of her quarterly vendor benchmarking, but as of 2026-05-06 she had not yet begun looking at Coupa. No update on Coupa engagement has been captured since. Procurement Express was named in prior sweep memory as a competitor but has not been mentioned on any call. The wedge against Tradogram is Zycus's full-service integration team and implementation support, which Steve positioned on 2026-05-06 and reinforced on 2026-06-16 as a key differentiator. Status of Coupa engagement and whether Thompson Pipe is sole-sourcing Zycus or running a competitive evaluation remains unknown as of 2026-06-29.

identify_pain.narrative: Carolyn Hartzell articulated specific operational pain on the 2026-05-06 discovery call: manual procurement processes causing inefficiency, lack of spend visibility across Thompson Pipe's multi-entity structure (California and Texas sites), inflexible approval workflows, and supplier document access control gaps (need for restricted data visibility by entity). The most acute pain was integration complexity: Tradogram (incumbent) required Thompson Pipe to lead integration scoping (60-page specification document), but Thompson Pipe's internal team could not define the integration points without external help, which Tradogram did not provide. This integration pain is the differentiation wedge Steve leveraged, positioning Zycus's full-service integration team as the solution. Carolyn owns the pain as Senior Purchasing Manager responsible for procurement process and vendor management across the organization.

decision_criteria.narrative: Thompson Pipe's evaluation criteria were articulated across the discovery and demo calls. On 2026-05-06, Carolyn outlined requirements: multi-entity reporting and approval workflows (California and Texas sites), supplier document access controls with restricted data visibility, Sage and Crexendo ERP integrations (critical pain point due to Tradogram's customer-led 60-page integration spec that Thompson Pipe could not execute without external help), and implementation support (positioned by Steve as Zycus full-service integration team advantage). On 2026-06-12 demo, Carolyn and attendees validated fit on approval workflow flexibility, multi-entity reporting, and supplier packet access. Post-demo (2026-06-16), Carolyn requested deeper technical discussion on these same points plus integration scoping, confirming these are the decision criteria. AI/Merlin positioning was present (products include Merlin, Merlin Intake, ANA) but no buyer-stated AI requirement or appetite captured on calls.

competitive_position.summary: Tradogram is the incumbent competitor and primary threat. Thompson Pipe is dissatisfied with Tradogram's customer-led integration approach (60-page spec they could not execute without external help), which is Zycus's differentiation wedge: full-service integration team and implementation support. Coupa was named on 2026-05-06 as a planned evaluation target but engagement status is unknown as of 2026-06-29. Procurement Express named in prior memory but not mentioned on any call. Competitive positioning is strong on integration and implementation support but not activated: no competitive review, no EB engagement to validate differentiation, no multi-threading to IT or finance stakeholders who would validate integration value. Risk: if Coupa engages and Zycus has not locked champion and Economic Buyer, Coupa brand recognition may overtake Zycus.
